output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Use This 5-Day Email Sequence
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Use this proven 5-day email sequence to recover your money fast—plus your tenant rights explained.
content_outline: Why Your Landlord Isn't Returning Your Deposit (And Why Emails Work)
Day 1: Send the Initial Request Email (Template Included)
Day 3: Follow Up Citing State Deadlines and Tenant Rights
Day 5: Send a Final Warning Before Formal Legal Action
What to Do When Emails Fail: Escalate to a Demand Letter
FAQ: What Can a Landlord Legally Deduct From Your Deposit?
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–30 days or provide an itemized deduction list
Normal wear and tear cannot be deducted; only actual damages beyond reasonable use are legally permissible
Email creates a paper trail, but a formal demand letter triggers specific legal deadlines and shows serious intent
If your landlord is wrongfully withholding your deposit, you may be entitled to double or triple damages depending on state law
output.cta: Stop waiting for your landlord to act. Generate a state-specific security deposit demand letter in minutes with DepositBack and demand what you're owed.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are wrongfully withholding, delaying, or refusing to return their security deposit
